title: Bridge Essay: Learning to Be Conscious.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Learning to Be Conscious.**. Trends Cogn Sci.. PMID: 31892458. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2019.11.011. Consciousness remains aformidable challenge. Different theories of consciousness have proposed vastly different mechanisms to account for phenomenal experience. Here, appealing to aspects of global workspace theory, higher-order theories, social theories, and predictive processing, we introduce anovel framework: the self-organizing metarerpresentational account (SOMA), in which consciousness is viewed as something that the brain learns to do.
